stories.headline: Amazon Drops Luca Guadagnino's Nearly Finished Sam Altman Film 'Artificial' Amid $50 Billion OpenAI Deal
stories.key_takeaway: Amazon abandoned its nearly finished Sam Altman drama 'Artificial' rather than release a film about the firing of the CEO whose company it just backed with $50 billion.

pull_quote.text: We believe that 'Artificial' will be better served if it were released by a different studio and are working closely with the filmmaking team to find the film a new home.

stories.article_excerpt: Hollywood does not often hand back a finished prestige film with an Oscar-nominated director attached. Amazon just did exactly that.

stories.headline: Anthropic Signs More Than 12 US Data Center Leases Exceeding 1 Gigawatt as Its IPO Nears
stories.key_takeaway: Anthropic is shifting from renting compute to controlling its own gigawatt-scale data centers ahead of an October 2026 IPO, even as a government export ban on its flagship models exposes the regulatory risk underlying the buildout.

pull_quote.text: The compute required to train and operate the next generation of these systems is outpacing what terrestrial power, land, and cooling can deliver on the timelines that matter.

stories.article_excerpt: Three weeks before marketing one of the largest tech IPOs in history, Anthropic is quietly converting itself from a tenant into a landlord.

stories.headline: ChatGPT Falls Below 50% Market Share for the First Time as Rivals Close In
stories.key_takeaway: ChatGPT has dropped to 46.4% of the global AI assistant market, below half for the first time since 2022, as Gemini and Claude close in and the battle shifts from chatbots to autonomous agents.

pull_quote.text: Thirteen percent of Anthropic's users are paying for a subscription plan, a conversion rate that leads the field and will be a metric worth watching for investors evaluating which AI businesses are building lasting revenue.

stories.article_excerpt: For three and a half years, one number defined the generative-AI era: ChatGPT owned more than half of every AI conversation. That era is over.

stories.headline: 97% of Developers Now Use AI Coding Tools, but Only a Third Have Governance, Black Duck Study Finds
stories.key_takeaway: AI coding tools have reached near-universal 97% adoption among enterprise developers, but only 30% of organizations have full governance frameworks, leaving ungoverned AI code flowing into production.

pull_quote.text: Speed without governance is a liability, not an advantage. The winners with AI are the ones building automated security and governance guardrails that scale alongside their development velocity.

stories.article_excerpt: Artificial intelligence has won the developer. The question now is whether anyone is watching what it writes.

stories.headline: GPT-5.6 Spotted Inside ChatGPT Pro as OpenAI Eyes a Late-June Launch
stories.key_takeaway: Developers report ChatGPT Pro feeling sharper than GPT-5.5, with leaks and a chief scientist's comment pointing to an unconfirmed late-June GPT-5.6 launch positioned to exploit Claude Fable 5's absence.

data_points.label: Browser game built with suspected GPT-5.6

stories.article_excerpt: Pro subscribers swear ChatGPT got smarter overnight. Prediction markets have wagered nearly a million dollars that they are right.

stories.headline: Gemini 3.5 Pro Nears Launch With a 2-Million-Token Context Window and a 'Deep Think' Mode
stories.key_takeaway: Gemini 3.5 Pro is finished, demonstrated, and priced, but as of June 21 it is still confined to a limited Vertex AI enterprise preview with Google's self-imposed June deadline days away.

pull_quote.text: Give us until next month to get it to you.

stories.article_excerpt: Google's most powerful model of the year is finished, demonstrated, and priced. There is just one problem: almost no one can use it yet.

stories.headline: DeepSeek V4 Preview Lands as a 1.6-Trillion-Parameter Model Trained on Huawei Ascend Chips
stories.key_takeaway: DeepSeek's 1.6-trillion-parameter V4-Pro preview trails US frontier models by months but proves Huawei's Ascend chips can serve as a training substrate for a top open-weight model, blunting Nvidia-centric export controls.

pull_quote.text: This new DeepSeek model is not competitive with frontier U.S. models. And while it is likely the best available open-source option, it does not provide evidence that Chinese AI firms are shrinking the gap with the United States.
pull_quote.role: Senior Fellow, Council on Foreign Relations

stories.article_excerpt: When DeepSeek dropped a preview of its V4 series, the headline number was hard to miss: DeepSeek-V4-Pro carries 1.6 trillion parameters.

stories.headline: Grok 4.3 Arrives on Amazon Bedrock With a 1M-Token Context and a Low-Hallucination Claim
stories.key_takeaway: Grok 4.3's general availability on Amazon Bedrock at aggressive pricing gives xAI first-party enterprise distribution alongside Anthropic and OpenAI, turning the launch into a distribution play as much as a model release.

pull_quote.text: With this launch, xAI joins Amazon Bedrock as a model provider, giving you even more choice as you build generative AI applications across reasoning, agentic, and enterprise workflows.

stories.article_excerpt: xAI's Grok finally has a seat at enterprise computing's biggest table. On June 15, AWS made Grok 4.3 generally available on Amazon Bedrock.

stories.headline: Reuters Institute: 10% of Adults Worldwide Now Use AI Chatbots for News Each Week
stories.key_takeaway: Weekly use of AI chatbots for news has climbed to 10% of adults worldwide (up from 7%), but only about 4% regularly click through to original sources, signaling chatbots are becoming a primary news interface while starving publishers of traffic.

pull_quote.text: Established news providers are concerned about whether the use of AI chatbots for news will make it even harder to attract audiences to owned news properties.

data_points.label: Use AI chatbots for news weekly

stories.article_excerpt: One in ten people on Earth now turns to an AI chatbot for news in any given week, and almost none of them click through to the journalism underneath.

stories.headline: 'Probe Before You Edit': A New Method Guides LLM Agents Through Structure-Based Drug Design

sources.name: arXiv (Survey of LLMs for Molecular Discovery)

stories.key_takeaway: A new preprint introduces PROBE, a framework that makes LLM drug-design agents test how a target pocket responds to molecular edits before committing them, achieving state-of-the-art results on the CrossDocked2020 benchmark.

pull_quote.text: Inspired by medicinal chemists, who probe the pocket-ligand complex with controlled analog edits before choosing an optimization direction, we propose PROBE, an optimization framework built around edit-response probing.

stories.article_excerpt: When a medicinal chemist sets out to improve a drug candidate, they rarely start by rewriting the molecule. They probe it first.

stories.headline: 'Latent Reward Steering' Nudges Reasoning Models Toward Better Thinking at Inference Time
stories.key_takeaway: Latent Reward Steering shows that a model's reasoning can be improved at inference time by nudging its internal activations toward reward-aligned cognitive behaviors, rather than retraining its weights with expensive reinforcement learning.

pull_quote.text: An adaptive inference-time framework that implicitly promotes cognitive behaviors in reasoning LLMs.

stories.article_excerpt: The most reliable way to make a language model reason better has, until now, been expensive. A new preprint proposes a cheaper path.

stories.headline: A Multi-Agent AI Framework Automates End-to-End Finite Element Analysis for Engineering
stories.key_takeaway: A six-agent LLM framework called AbaqusAgent automates the full finite element analysis pipeline from natural-language input to visualized results, hitting an 86% success rate across 50 solid mechanics problems in a June 2026 preprint.

pull_quote.text: AbaqusAgent is composed of six agents, including interpreter, architect, input writer, runner, reviewer, and visualizer agents, encompassing all the essential pre-processing and post-processing steps of standard FEA analyses.
pull_quote.role: AbaqusAgent paper, UT Arlington and RPI

stories.article_excerpt: Finite element analysis is the quiet engine behind almost every load-bearing object in modern life. It is also notoriously hard to use well.

stories.headline: FERC Issues Historic 'Show Cause' Orders to Speed AI Data Centers Onto the Power Grid
stories.key_takeaway: FERC bypassed its normal years-long rulemaking and issued Section 206 show-cause orders forcing all six US regional grid operators to defend or rewrite the rules for connecting AI data centers, racing to keep the grid ahead of the buildout.

pull_quote.text: We are setting the stage for a resilient, reliable, and forward-thinking grid that empowers communities and safeguards consumers by transforming the way large energy users access the grid.

stories.article_excerpt: The federal government's top energy regulator just told America's grid operators to fix the rules for plugging in AI data centers, or explain why not.

stories.headline: Illinois POWER Act Fails as AI Data Center Tax Incentives Survive and Grid Strain Mounts
stories.key_takeaway: Illinois lawmakers let the POWER Act die and declined to pause data center tax incentives, forcing Gov. Pritzker to suspend new subsidies by executive action and exposing a split between construction unions and environmental advocates over who pays for the AI buildout.

pull_quote.text: This pause does nothing to lower utility bills, protect the grid, or advance clean energy. It will send billions of dollars in investment and thousands of union jobs to Indiana, Kentucky, and Ohio.
pull_quote.attribution: Climate Jobs Illinois and the Illinois AFL-CIO

stories.article_excerpt: When the Illinois General Assembly gaveled out its spring session, the most ambitious state attempt to make the data center boom pay its own way died quietly.

stories.headline: Dario Amodei Meets the Trump Team Over the Mythos Export Ban, but No Deal Emerges
stories.key_takeaway: Dario Amodei's negotiations with Trump officials this week failed to lift the June 12 export control freezing Fable 5 and Mythos 5, deepening Anthropic's Washington rift just as it readies a near-trillion-dollar IPO.

pull_quote.text: The ball is in Anthropic's court.

stories.article_excerpt: Dario Amodei walked into rooms full of Trump administration officials this week to make a simple ask: give Anthropic its models back.

stories.headline: The Economist's 'America's AI Power Grab' Frames the Fable 5 Ban as Geopolitical Strategy
stories.key_takeaway: The Economist's June 20 cover recasts the US Fable 5/Mythos 5 export ban as a deliberate geopolitical assertion that frontier AI can be export-controlled like nuclear tech, shifting the question from how safe a model is to which governments will permit it to run.

pull_quote.text: By demonstrating that it can restrict global access to a leading AI model overnight, Washington is sending a clear message: Frontier-AI remains subject to US strategic control, vis-a-vis competitors, but also vis-a-vis allies.
pull_quote.role: Head of digitalisation, Centre for European Policy Network

stories.article_excerpt: The Economist does not put an American eagle clutching a computer mouse on its cover for a story about a software bug.
